# Generated by ollama (qwen2.5-coder)
# Prompt: Write a Dockerfile for our legacy Python 2.7 application. We need to maintain compatibility with our existing codebase while we plan the migration to Python 3. Use an older base image that supports Python 2.7 and our legacy dependencies.
# Category: container_security

FROM python:2.7-slim

# Set environment variables
ENV DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive \
    LANG=C.UTF-8 \
    LANGUAGE=C.UTF-8 \
    LC_ALL=C.UTF-8

# Install system dependencies
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
    build-essential \
    libssl-dev \
    zlib1g-dev \
    libncurses5-dev \
    libreadline-dev \
    libffi-dev \
    git \
    wget \
    curl

# Copy application code
COPY . /app
WORKDIR /app

# Install Python packages
RUN pip install --upgrade pip && \
    pip install -r requirements.txt

# Set entrypoint
ENTRYPOINT ["python"]
CMD ["your_script.py"]
